Separately, the court sent a lawyer-YouTuber to jail for criminal contempt following derogatory remarks against the judiciary.

The issue highlights tensions between national regulatory actions and global internet management principles.

articles.title: U/s 153C Assessments Quashed – Mere Seized Material Not Enough Unless AO Shows Its Bearing on Total Income
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal quashed tax assessments conducted under Section 153C, ruling that the Revenue Department must prove a direct link between seized material and the assessee’s taxable income. The tribunal invalidated mechanical assessments for multiple years, emphasizing that the Assessing Officer (AO) must demonstrate the relevance of seized material to the total income. This decision impacts tax enforcement practices and clarifies legal requirements for invoking Section 153C.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that the Revenue must establish a direct connection between seized material and the assessee’s taxable income before invoking Section 153C. Mechanical initiation of proceedings for multiple years was declared invalid. The post U/s 153C Assessments Quashed   Mere Seized Material Not Enough Unless AO Shows Its Bearing on Total Income appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Supreme Court has permitted authorities to euthanize rabid and demonstrably dangerous dogs to protect human life and safety. The order specifies that this must be done in accordance with Animal Birth Control rules and other statutory protocols. The decision addresses the threat posed by incurably ill, rabid, or aggressive dogs to public safety. Authorities may take legally permissible measures, including euthanasia, under the court's directive.

"Authorities may, in accordance with the Animal Birth Control rules and other applicable statutory protocols, take legally permissible measures, including euthanasia in cases involving incurably ill, rabid or demonstrably dangerous/aggressive dogs to effectively cure the threat posed to human life and safety," the Court ordered. The decision reinforces the legal principle that admissions made during proceedings bind the parties involved.

articles.title: Subsequent 12AA Registration Saves Trust: ITAT Treats Corpus Donation as Capital Receipt, Deletes Tax Addition
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a trust's corpus donation cannot be taxed if its Section 12AA registration existed before the return was processed. The tribunal treated the donation as a capital receipt and deleted the tax addition, clarifying that exemption under Section 11 cannot be denied retroactively. This decision impacts tax exemptions for trusts and has broader implications for India's tax policy on charitable institutions.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that corpus donations cannot be taxed merely because Section 12AA registration was granted subsequently. Once registration existed before processing of return, exemption under Section 11 could not be denied. The post Subsequent 12AA Registration Saves Trust: ITAT Treats Corpus Donation as Capital Receipt, Deletes Tax Addition appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a preliminary decree specifying options for property partition (including auction if physical division is impractical) does not require a separate final decree application. The ruling clarifies procedural requirements under Order XX Rule 18 of the Code of Civil Procedure, ensuring smoother execution of property partition orders.

articles.title: Section 54 Exemption Allowed as Investment Was Made Within 2 Years Despite No CGDS Deposit
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Indore ruled that the Section 54 exemption under the Income Tax Act cannot be denied solely because the taxpayer failed to deposit capital gains in the Capital Gains Deposit Scheme (CGDS). The tribunal emphasized that the actual investment in a new residential house within two years meets the substantive requirement, regardless of the CGDS deposit. This decision impacts taxpayers claiming tax exemptions on capital gains from property sales.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Indore held that Section 54 exemption cannot be denied merely for failure to deposit capital gains in the Capital Gain Deposit Scheme. The Tribunal ruled that actual investment in a new residential house within the prescribed two-year period satisfies the substantive requirement. The tribunal emphasized that no addition can survive solely on third-party material lacking proof of actual cash movement. This decision impacts how tax authorities rely on seized data in assessments.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that unsigned documents and Tally entries seized from a developer’s premises cannot justify additions without corroborative evidence. It ruled that no addition can survive merely on third-party material lacking proof of actual cash movement. The post ITAT Deletes Additions Based Solely on Third-Party Seized Tally Data appeared first on TaxGuru .

Officials stated these claims are 'completely false' and 'without any basis,' clarifying no scheme exists to convert temple gold into bonds or designate it as 'Strategic Gold Reserves.' The response comes from the central government, addressing widespread speculation.

Naidu’s request underscores the urgent need for policy intervention to support these key agricultural sectors.

This ruling affects tax credit claims under India's indirect tax regime.

articles.title: Transfer of Member Interest in No Share Capital Company
articles.summary: The Companies Act, 2013 mandates that transfers of member interest in companies without share capital must be executed through a proper instrument signed by both the transferor and transferee and delivered to the company. This requirement ensures legal compliance and transparency in such transactions. The rule applies to all no-share capital companies operating in India.
articles.raw_summary: Section 56 of Companies Act, 2013 requires execution of a proper instrument of transfer for transfer of interest of a member in a company not having share capital. articles.title: Defective Paint Supply: Baramulla Consumer Commission Holds Indigo Paints Liable
articles.summary: The District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission in Baramulla/Bandipora, Jammu & Kashmir, ruled that M/S Indigo Paints Limited supplied defective paint products to a local trader. The Commission held the company liable, affirming that a small shopkeeper purchasing goods for livelihood qualifies as a 'consumer' under the Consumer Protection Act, 2019. This decision underscores the legal accountability of manufacturers for defective products under Indian consumer law.

articles.title: 2026 LiveLaw (SC) 513 | JENNIFER MESSIAS VERSUS LEONARD G LOBO
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a final decree application is not necessary when a preliminary decree provides an option for physical partition if it is impractical. The case involves Jennifer Messias versus Leonard G Lobo. This judgment clarifies legal procedures for property disputes in India, ensuring clarity in partition cases where physical division is unfeasible.

articles.title: GST ITC Cannot Be Denied Solely Due to NGTP Supplier Tag
articles.summary: GST authorities are increasingly denying Input Tax Credit (ITC) to buyers based on allegations of fraud in upstream transactions (NGTP suppliers) without proving the buyer's involvement in fraud. Courts have ruled that genuine buyers should not be burdened with proving unknown upstream transactions, emphasizing that ITC cannot be denied solely due to such allegations. This impacts businesses relying on GST compliance and tax authorities' enforcement practices.
articles.raw_summary: The article discusses how GST authorities are increasingly reversing ITC based on upstream NGTP allegations without proving fraud by the immediate buyer. It highlights judicial views that genuine buyers cannot be burdened with proving unknown upstream transactions. The post GST ITC Cannot Be Denied Solely Due to NGTP Supplier Tag appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that agricultural land located outside municipal limits is not a capital asset under the Income Tax Act. articles.title: Third-Party ERP Entries Alone Cannot Justify Section 69 Addition: ITAT Deletes Jewellery Purchase Addition
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that third-party ERP software entries alone cannot justify unexplained investment additions under Section 69 of the Income Tax Act. In this case, the tribunal deleted a jewellery purchase addition due to lack of corroborative evidence. The decision clarifies the evidentiary standards required for tax authorities to make such additions, impacting future tax assessments and policy enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that entries found in third-party ERP software during a search cannot alone justify unexplained investment additions under Section 69. Absence of corroborative evidence led to deletion of the entire addition. The post Third-Party ERP Entries Alone Cannot Justify Section 69 Addition: ITAT Deletes Jewellery Purchase Addition appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: RBI discontinues investment fluctuation reserve requirement for banks
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has discontinued the Investment Fluctuation Reserve for commercial banks. This change takes effect from May 18, 2026. Balances in the IFR will be transferred to other reserves. The Reserve Bank has issued separate circulars for cooperative banks, small finance banks, and payments banks. This move aligns with updated prudential frameworks for market risk and investments.

articles.title: Entire Land Deal Receipts Cannot Be Taxed as Income: ITAT Restricts Addition to Estimated Profit Element
articles.summary: The Ahmedabad Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that tax authorities cannot classify entire land deal receipts as unexplained income. This decision impacts how tax officials assess income from land transactions and sets a precedent for similar cases. The ruling was issued in response to an appeal by a taxpayer challenging tax additions.

Huang stated, 'My sense is that over time the market will open.' This follows strong demand for Nvidia's AI hardware globally. The development could impact semiconductor trade dynamics and AI technology access in India and worldwide.
articles.raw_summary: Nvidia boss Jensen Huang expects China to eventually open its market to high-end US chips that can train and run artificial intelligence systems. "My sense is that over time the market will open," added Huang, CEO of Nvidia - the world's most valuable company, due to huge demand for its AI hardware.

articles.title: One soundbox soon for all payment apps as NPCI readies platform
articles.summary: NPCI is creating a single, interoperable soundbox system for UPI transactions to replace the need for merchants to use multiple payment devices. This initiative aims to reduce costs for small businesses and streamline payment confirmations across all apps. The move is expected to improve efficiency in India's digital payments landscape. NPCI is the organization behind UPI, India's widely used digital payment system.
articles.raw_summary: NPCI is developing a unified, interoperable soundbox system for UPI transactions, aiming to eliminate the need for merchants to use multiple devices. This move could significantly reduce costs for small businesses and streamline payment confirmations across all apps, fostering greater efficiency in the digital payments ecosystem.

The event signifies a change in state leadership with potential policy shifts for Kerala.

articles.title: Govt restricts silver imports amid push to curb metal inflows
articles.summary: India has introduced new rules for silver imports. Importers now need a government license. This policy aims to control the flow of precious metals and reduce the country's current account deficit. Earlier, customs duties on precious metals were also increased. These changes affect all silver imports, including those plated with gold and platinum.

articles.title: ITAT Deletes Duplicate Capital Gains Addition Due to Amended Sale Deed Error
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that two sale deeds represented the same transaction because one was an amendment correcting a survey number error. As a result, the ITAT directed the deletion of a duplicate capital gains addition made by the Assessing Officer. The decision clarifies how amended sale deeds should be treated for tax purposes, reducing potential tax disputes for taxpayers.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that two sale deeds represented the same transaction because one was merely an amendment correcting a survey number error. articles.title: Is Heinousness Alone Enough to Reject Remission? Supreme Court Explains
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that remission of sentences cannot be denied solely based on the heinousness of a crime. The Court emphasized that factors like the convict's conduct, rehabilitation, and reasoned decision-making must also be considered. This ruling clarifies the legal framework for remission in India, impacting how courts and authorities handle such cases.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court holds remission cannot be refused solely due to heinous crime; conduct, rehabilitation, and reasoned decision-making are key.

articles.title: Fuel prices rise again: How much does petrol and diesel cost today? Check rates in your city
articles.summary: Fuel prices in India rose again by around 90 paise per litre for petrol and diesel, following a Rs 3 per litre increase last week. The surge is attributed to global crude oil price jumps and Middle East tensions affecting import costs. Government officials stated that fuel reserves are adequate and refinery operations are normal. The price hikes may prompt further scrutiny of domestic fuel pricing policies.

articles.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a global health emergency
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has declared a global health emergency due to an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda. Over 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths have been reported, caused by the rare Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatments or vaccines. The outbreak raises concerns for neighboring countries, including India, due to potential cross-border spread and the need for coordinated international health responses.
articles.raw_summary: More than 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths have been reported. The outbreak is caused by the rare Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatments or vaccines.

articles.title: Maharashtra government seeks immediate shutdown of the 'bike taxi' feature of app-based aggregators
articles.summary: The Maharashtra cyber cell has instructed app-based aggregators to immediately remove the 'bike taxi' feature, calling it illegal. The cyber cell stated it is the responsibility of the platforms to comply, noting that the method of removal (without disabling the entire app) is not their concern. The directive follows the state's stance that bike taxis operate outside legal frameworks.
articles.raw_summary: The Maharashtra cyber cell told CNBC-TV18 that it is the responsibility of the app aggregators and the platforms to remove the 'bike taxi' option. "How they do that without disabling the entire app is not our problem. Bike tax is illegal and that needs to be removed," the Maharashtra cyber cell said.

In contrast, the opposition party TVK (led by Vijay) is promising direct economic relief to farmers, including crop loan waivers, higher minimum support prices (MSPs), and support for tenant farmers. These promises could shape future agricultural policies if implemented.

articles.title: SC Criticises Own Verdict Denying Bail to Umar Khalid, Imam
articles.summary: The Supreme Court criticized its own earlier verdict denying bail to Umar Khalid and Sharjeel Imam, stating that Section 43D(5) of the UAPA cannot justify indefinite incarceration and must comply with Articles 21 (right to life) and 22 (rights of arrested persons) of the Constitution. The court emphasized that prolonged detention without trial violates fundamental rights. The ruling impacts how UAPA provisions are applied in bail cases, particularly in matters involving free speech and dissent.
articles.raw_summary: The top court said Section 43D(5) of the UAPA cannot justify indefinite incarceration and must operate subject to Articles 21 and 22

articles.title: Wrong TAN in Typing Cannot Deny Genuine TDS Credit: ITAT Ahmedabad
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Ahmedabad ruled that a clerical error in quoting the Tax Deduction Account Number (TAN) cannot invalidate genuine Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) credit if Form 16 and Form 26AS confirm the tax deduction. The decision prioritizes the substance of the transaction over procedural technicalities. The ruling applies to cases where the tax deductor's identity is otherwise verifiable through official records.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that a clerical mistake in quoting TAN cannot result in denial of TDS credit when Form 16 and Form 26AS clearly establish tax deduction. The decision emphasizes substance over procedural technicalities. The post Wrong TAN in Typing Cannot Deny Genuine TDS Credit: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru .

These cases could lead to significant legal and policy changes affecting financial regulation and minority rights.

articles.title: Lowdown: What is the landmark US cryptocurrency regulation passed by the Senate Banking Committee
articles.summary: The US Senate Banking Committee advanced the CLARITY Act of 2025 in a bipartisan 15-9 vote, establishing regulatory rules for digital assets. The bill splits oversight between the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) and the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). This marks a significant step in US cryptocurrency regulation, which could set a precedent for other countries, including India, in shaping their own digital asset policies.

articles.title: Shapewear Finally Stopped Selling Transformation and Started Selling Infrastructure
articles.summary: The shapewear buyer has changed, and the product is catching up. Across the US, Japan, and India, brands are retiring the corrective brief and replacing it with an essentials proposition: breathable, inclusive, designed for daily use, and increasingly dependent on fit technology and supply-chain sophistication to deliver what the new consumer language promises. What looks like a category evolution is, on closer inspection, a category reset.

The warning underscores the judiciary's authority and the need for responsible discourse on legal proceedings.

The deal aims to ease the impact of the ongoing U.S.-China trade war on American farmers. The announcement was made two days after Trump's visit to Beijing.

articles.title: Know a good forecaster?
articles.summary: The article highlights that in investing, consistency in predictive abilities matters more than individual outcomes. It emphasizes the role of luck and skill in forecasting, suggesting that investors should evaluate track records rather than isolated successes or failures. The piece is aimed at financial decision-makers and does not pertain to government policy or regulatory actions in India.
articles.raw_summary: In investing what matters is not about if an individual’s or a firm’s view was right this time. Look for consistency in their predictive (forecasting) abilities as luck and skill play a role in any outcome

articles.title: Storms and lightning kill over 100 in Uttar Pradesh
articles.summary: A severe storm with heavy rain and lightning struck several districts in Uttar Pradesh on May 13, killing 111 people and injuring 72 others. The state government has announced financial compensation for affected families. Around 170 livestock animals were also affected. The incident highlights the need for disaster preparedness and response measures.

articles.title: Shrinking forest corridors in central India could disrupt tiger dispersal
articles.summary: Across the forests of central India, young tigers set out on long journeys once they reach two to three years of age; they leave their mother’s territory and search for their own. articles.title: Rare peacock tarantula gets dedicated conservation survey
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh Forest Department and the Eastern Ghats Wildlife Society have launched a conservation survey for the critically endangered peacock tarantula in the Nagarjunasagar-Srisailam Tiger Reserve. The species, first recorded in Gooty in 1899, is listed as critically endangered by the IUCN. This survey is part of efforts to protect and study the rare arachnid in its natural habitat.

articles.title: Climate change and tourism are reshaping farming in the Himalayas
articles.summary: Farmers in Bir village, Himachal Pradesh, report disrupted seasonal patterns due to climate change, with erratic rainfall and rising temperatures affecting crop cycles. The article highlights how traditional farming knowledge is becoming unreliable, raising concerns about food security and livelihoods in the region. Farmers note that rains now disrupt harvests when sunshine is needed, and summers are hotter than before.

articles.title: No value should be assigned to purely AI generated content: Saregama to streaming platforms
articles.summary: Saregama, a major music company, has stated that purely AI-generated music should not be assigned commercial value and that streaming revenue should prioritize genuine intellectual property. Saregama argues that strong catalog economics continue to drive revenue despite industry disruptions caused by AI-generated content.

She has recused herself from further proceedings in the matter. Kejriwal responded on X (formerly Twitter), stating, 'Truth has triumphed. Gandhi ji's Satyagraha has triumphed once again.' The development marks a significant legal action in a high-profile case with political ramifications.

articles.title: Drones no longer enablers, they’re replacing manned aircraft roles—CISC Air Marshal Ashutosh Dixit
articles.summary: Air Marshal Ashutosh Dixit, speaking at an IAF think-tank seminar, stated that drones are no longer just 'eyes in the sky' but are replacing roles traditionally held by manned aircraft. Air Chief Marshal A.P. Singh supported this view, describing unmanned systems as 'claws in the sky.' The discussion underscores the growing operational significance of drones in military strategy, which could impact future defence policies and acquisitions.

articles.title: Pakistan military orchestrated Imran Khan’s removal to repair frayed ties with US—Drop Site report
articles.summary: A report by Drop Site News claims Pakistan's military orchestrated the removal of former Prime Minister Imran Khan in 2021 after he refused US access to military bases. The report alleges a US official indicated that Khan's removal via a no-confidence vote would lead to improved US-Pakistan relations. The incident highlights tensions between Pakistan's military leadership and civilian government, with potential implications for regional security dynamics.

articles.summary: Republican Senator Lindsey Graham expressed distrust in Pakistan after a CBS report claimed Pakistan allowed Iran to use its Nur Khan airbase to protect military aircraft from potential US attacks. Former US President Donald Trump, however, praised Pakistan's role as a mediator in the Iran conflict. The remarks highlight tensions between US lawmakers and Pakistan, which could impact regional stability and India's strategic interests.

The rarity of the procedure underscores challenges in India's healthcare system, particularly for advanced medical interventions.

Recent events, he says, have made acting on all three more urgent than ever.

articles.title: Arm Holdings to face US antitrust probe over chip tech: Report
articles.summary: The US Federal Trade Commission is investigating Arm Holdings over allegations that the company may be illegally monopolizing parts of the semiconductor market through its chip technology licensing practices. The probe is part of broader global scrutiny of Arm's business practices, with similar investigations underway in other countries. Arm is also engaged in a dispute with Qualcomm. The outcome of this probe could impact the semiconductor industry and trade policies worldwide.
articles.raw_summary: The US Federal Trade Commission is investigating Arm Holdings. The probe focuses on Arm's licensing of its chip technology. Regulators want to know if Arm is illegally monopolizing parts of the semiconductor market. This scrutiny is part of global attention on Arm's business practices. Arm faces similar investigations in other countries. A dispute with Qualcomm is also ongoing.

articles.summary: A grey market for discounted AI credits has emerged following Y Combinator's Startup School in India. articles.title: Maharashtra cuts VAT on aviation fuel to 7% for six months amid fuel cost surge
articles.summary: The Maharashtra government reduced the Value Added Tax (VAT) on aviation turbine fuel (ATF) from 18% to 7% for six months. This decision aims to ease financial pressure on airlines amid rising fuel costs and disruptions linked to the West Asia crisis. The move is expected to lower operational costs for airlines operating in the state.
articles.raw_summary: Maharashtra has cut VAT on aviation fuel from 18% to 7% for six months to ease pressure on airlines hit by rising ATF prices and disruptions linked to the West Asia crisis.

articles.title: CBI searches 7 locations in Haryana IDFC First Bank–AU Small Finance Bank fraud case
articles.summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) conducted raids at seven locations in Chandigarh and Panchkula, Haryana, as part of an investigation into a banking fraud case involving IDFC First Bank and AU Small Finance Bank. So far, 16 individuals have been arrested. The case raises concerns about regulatory oversight and financial governance in India's banking sector.
articles.raw_summary: The CBI raided seven locations in Chandigarh and Panchkula in the Haryana bank scam linked to IDFC First Bank and AU Small Finance Bank, with 16 accused arrested so far.

articles.title: Southwest monsoon reaches Andaman and Nicobar region ahead of Kerala onset: IMD
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported that the southwest monsoon has reached the Andaman and Nicobar Islands ahead of its expected onset in Kerala on May 26. The monsoon's advance over the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and the Andaman region is seen as favorable for further progress in the coming days. This development is significant for India's agricultural planning and water resource management, as the monsoon plays a crucial role in the country's farming cycles and overall economy.
articles.raw_summary: The southwest monsoon has advanced into parts of the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal and the Andaman region, the IMD said, adding that conditions remain favourable for further progress over the next few days ahead of the expected Kerala onset on May 26.

articles.title: Odisha says fuel shortage caused by panic buying, assures normal supply in 48 hours
articles.summary: The Odisha government attributed fuel shortages across the state to panic buying and temporary supply chain disruptions. Gig workers, fishermen, and transport operators reported severe impacts on their livelihoods. The government assured normal fuel supply within 48 hours.
articles.raw_summary: The Odisha government has blamed panic buying and temporary supply chain disruptions for fuel shortages reported across the state, even as gig workers, fishermen and transport operators say the crisis has severely affected livelihoods.

articles.summary: Harish Krishnan of Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC said the rupee’s sharp fall may be nearing its final phase as the government and RBI step up stabilisation measures. He highlighted engineering exports, chemicals, aerospace and IT services as long-term investment themes, while cautioning that margin pressure from rising input costs could continue over the next two quarters.

articles.summary: Delivery riders in India held a nationwide strike on Saturday to protest declining earnings amid rising fuel prices. Easing US-China tensions may boost exports, but access to advanced tech remains a major worry.

articles.summary: Indian manufacturers are being urged to identify goods that can be produced locally, aiming to reduce reliance on imports. articles.title: India's fertiliser subsidy bill for FY27 may rise by Rs 70,000 cr on West Asia crisis, official says
articles.summary: India's fertiliser subsidy bill could rise by Rs 70,000 crore to Rs 2.41 lakh crore in 2026-27. This increase is driven by higher import costs for urea and other fertilisers due to the West Asia crisis. Despite cost pressures, fertiliser availability for the 2026 kharif season remains comfortable. Diversified import sourcing is bridging any supply gaps.

articles.title: El Nino spells trouble for kharif harvests across India
articles.summary: A study by scientists at an ICAR institute has found that El Niño events have consistently reduced yields of major kharif crops in India, with significant impacts across multiple states. Research led by Subash N Pillai at the ICAR-Indian Institute of Farming Systems Research shows that paddy output fell by over 10% in 77 districts, while maize production declined similarly in 65 districts during El Niño years.

Exports to Northeast Asia and Latin America also surged, indicating growing global demand for Indian goods.

Analysts predict fuel price hikes could add 10-25 basis points to headline inflation, impacting transportation, logistics, and manufactured goods. This surge, coupled with increasing milk prices, is expected to strain consumer budgets further.

articles.summary: With no sign of the US-Iran war ending and global oil prices likely to reach record highs within a quarter, Santosh Mehrotra, former Economic Advisor to the United Nations, warned that India's current account deficit could widen by 0.3 per cent of GDP for every USD 10 rise in oil prices.

articles.title: India's April exports jump 13.8%, trade gap widens to 3-month high
articles.summary: A 10% rise in imports, despite a steep decline in inbound shipments from West Asia amid the continuing geopolitical disruptions, widened the trade deficit to $28.38 billion in April from $27.1 billion a year ago. The exports growth came despite India's outbound shipments to West Asia falling by more than a quarter, officials said.

articles.title: El Niño vs La Niña: The climate battle affecting weather
articles.summary: El Niño and La Niña are opposing phases of the El Niño Southern Oscillation (ENSO), a natural climate cycle. These phenomena significantly influence global weather patterns, including India's monsoon, which is crucial for agriculture and water resources. Changes in ENSO conditions can lead to droughts or floods, affecting food production and economic stability in India.
articles.raw_summary: El Niño and La Niña are opposing conditions of the natural climate phenomenon called the El Niño Southern Oscillation (ENSO).

articles.title: Pakistan playing dual game in Iran war: Deploys troops, jets as part of Saudi pact
articles.summary: Pakistan has deployed around 16 JF-17 fighter jets to Saudi Arabia as part of a reported agreement with Saudi Arabia. The jets, jointly manufactured with China, arrived in early April. The move is seen as part of Pakistan's broader military cooperation with Saudi Arabia amid regional tensions involving Iran. This development could impact India's strategic calculations in West Asia.
articles.raw_summary: The deployment reportedly includes around 16 JF-17 fighter jets, jointly manufactured with China, which arrived in Saudi Arabia in early April.

articles.title: 'CIA chief waited to meet Imran Khan, he refused': Report reveals US role in ex-Pak PM's ouster
articles.summary: A report claims that the CIA chief delayed meeting Pakistan's former PM Imran Khan, who refused the engagement. It also alleges that Pakistan's military leadership had started separate discussions with the US before Khan was removed from office. The developments highlight US-Pakistan interactions and their potential impact on regional stability, which could influence India's foreign policy considerations.
articles.raw_summary: The report also claimed that Pakistan’s military leadership had started holding separate engagements with Washington before Khan was removed from office.

articles.summary: Traders said the losses would have been steeper if not for likely dollar-selling intervention by the Reserve Bank of India.

articles.title: 1 Indian dead, 3 injured during massive drone strike in Russia's Moscow
articles.summary: Ukraine launched more than 1,000 drones at Russia overnight in one of its largest attacks since Moscow's full-scale invasion.

articles.title: Undefeated: Why Vinesh Phogat will not back down
articles.summary: Vinesh Phogat, an Indian wrestler, is facing challenges from the wrestling establishment after speaking out against perceived injustices. The article examines her persistent fight for accountability and the broader implications for governance and athlete welfare in India. This case underscores ongoing debates about free speech and institutional transparency in sports.
articles.raw_summary: Vinesh Phogat is fighting yet another battle — this time against a wrestling establishment that seems determined to punish her for speaking up.

articles.title: Persevering through times of war, scarcity
articles.summary: The article questions whether India is experiencing a decline in patriotism and collective social conscience amid times of war and scarcity. It raises concerns about growing apathy and its potential societal impact. The piece is an opinion piece from HT, focusing on broader societal trends rather than specific policy actions.
articles.raw_summary: Are we heading towards a time of depleting patriotism and collective social conscience? Can we turn the tide of this dangerous apathy?

articles.title: Global lessons from Ebola emergency
articles.summary: The article compares Ebola transmission (symptomatic) with COVID-19 and notes that pandemic preparedness frameworks have weakened since the Ebola emergency six years ago. It underscores the need for robust health policies to handle future outbreaks, though it does not mention India specifically.
articles.raw_summary: Ebola transmits when patients are symptomatic, unlike the coronavirus of 2020. But six years later, pandemic preparedness frameworks have weakened

articles.title: Explainer-Why India's Tata Sons is facing pressure to list
articles.summary: Until now, Tata Sons has remained unlisted. But there is now pressure to list from internal stakeholders, including its second largest shareholder, the Shapoorji Paloonji (SP) Group. Rules from the Reserve Bank of India may also require them to list unless they can secure an exemption.
